title: Recall uses low-weight local sparse vector scoring
summary: Kage recall now computes a local sparse vector cosine score over packet title, summary, tags, paths, type, and body. The score is low weighted so it is inspectable in score breakdown.vector without overpowering BM25, tem
body:
Kage recall now computes a local sparse-vector cosine score over packet title, summary, tags, paths, type, and body. The score is low-weighted so it is inspectable in score_breakdown.vector without overpowering BM25, temporal expansion, semantic concept expansion, graph, freshness, or quality. A higher vector weight regressed LongMemEval-S R@10 to 99.79%, so the weight is intentionally conservative.
Evidence: Implemented scorePacketsVector in mcp/kernel.ts and added vector assertions to recall concept expansion tests in mcp/kernel.test.ts. Updated benchmark docs to describe local sparse-vector scoring.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp; node benchmarks/longmemeval-kage-retrieval.mjs --data /private/tmp/kage-external-bench/longmemeval_s_cleaned.json --limit 470 --top-k 10 --out /private/tmp/kage-external-bench/longmemeval-kage-full-vector-local-lowweight.json
